reviews.review_description: Hand tracking only option makes this game impossible to enjoy. The hand tracking is horrible and my right hand fires constantly even when my fingers are far apart. There should definitely be a "use controllers" option. I really wanted to enjoy this one.

reviews.review_title: Worthy Iconic Title for the Quest
reviews.review_description:
This retro game brought to modern day gaming is tricky but fun. The weapon choices are adequate and you do have to plan and lead your targets although you only have a brief time especially when multiple missiles are raining down.

But if you are quick and smart than you can make it to the top 5 in the global leaderboards like me. 

One update request I have is that they include controller support.

developer_response.body: Thank you for the kind words & congrats on TOP 5 on the Leaderboards! 😁 Controller support is definitely coming - along with a few other improvements - stay tuned!

reviews.review_description:
The game looks so good in headset the neon colors really pops.

Dear developers please add controller support, its unplayable with handtracking for me, only 30% of pinches are detected.

developer_response.body: Thank you! :) Yes, we are planning to add controller support (just like we did on our other game, Banners & Bastions). Stay tuned for updates!

reviews.review_title: 🛡️ Protect the Skies in MR and VR
reviews.review_description:
Airspace Defender is a beautifully crafted and surprisingly intense air defense game playable in both MR and VR. Using full hand tracking, you command ground defense units with precision — pinching your fingers to launch missiles at incoming rockets threatening civilians or military bases.

The game demands focus and quick reflexes as enemies attack from multiple directions, forcing you to stay alert and strategic. The explosions are visually stunning (almost too mesmerizing for a war zone), but there’s no time to admire them — another wave is already incoming!

Upgrades and different enemy types keep the gameplay fresh, and the variety of missions ensures strong replayability. It’s an immersive experience that blends tension, precision, and satisfaction — a must-try for anyone who loves defense or hand-tracking-based games.

In my experience, many VR games get old quickly  for various reasons. This one is great! Simple concept, very fun, and worth the cost. Main problem is sometimes it doesn't recognize my hand pinching motion, even after multiple, which is frustrating, but it's not bad enough to lose a star. Would definitely play more if that was improved. Also would appreciate a little more description of the weapon options. Overall, I love the design and simplicity of it.

reviews.review_title: Yes! A mix of complexity and simplicity
reviews.review_description:
I love this kind of game play. Arcade like with attention to detail and quality. Must have addition to my library. 

Requests: 

● The addition to play in my Meta virtual home, not only passthrough.

● Please offer other spatial immersive environments other than a black void. BUT please don't cheap out with AI slop. I hate when a Sky dome/ box is blurry and looks cheap because it breaks immersion. 

● Please allow me to pinch, drag and enlarge the size of the Airspace Defender Dome Battle Arena. I like the tabletop size, but I'd also like to play this standing at Roomscale at least 5X the size of the current battle dome.

● A multiplayer lobby where you can join in on a 1 v. 1 match between two roles: either the Airspace Defender or the Airspace Attacker. Include private matches where you can be invited to join plus a public lobby. 
   -That would be exceptional if you could add Co-Play + Co-Presence so my opponent's Meta avatar would be in my living room on the other side of the dome.
*Please make this happen! 🙏🏻

Great Mixed Reality!

Highly recommend and agree with all the other great reviews….. 

Keep adding more content.

Thanks!

If you are an old head like me and enjoyed missile command from back in the day, this game is for you!  It is quite challenging and can be frustrating because after the first few levels, the attacks keep coming.  You do need to play this in a well lit room or near a light in order for your hands to appear to launch your defenses.
Fun game. Recommended.

My first game purchases were lackluster until I found this Missile Command like thriller! I loved Missile Command from back in the day and this takes it to another dimension. I also love that it is.mixed reality ,which makes it better. They did a tremendous job on this.

I’m usually not a fan of hand tracking games because one single time your gesture doesn’t register it could mean game over. The hand tracking (pinching to be more precise) is as close to perfect as I’ve ever seen. It’s one of those games you find yourself getting sucked into and sayin “just 1 more run”… I love the upgrades, I love the challenge, I love everything about it. Hope you’ll add some more turrets soon, but honestly, that’s just me being greedy. Get this now. These are the type of developers we need more of… great job to yall. Can’t wait to see what the future holds for this one
Amazing , 👏
Add an easy mode because it's too hard and people quickly lose interest in the game. If you do, I'll give it five stars.

reviews.review_title: This is a GOOD little game!
reviews.review_description:
I played Missile Commandin the arcades, AND on the Atari, and I can tell you this is a worthy remake and translates brilliantly for VR. Cheers 👍

Paid $10 on sale, and it was the right price. The mechanics are there, but it’s a little “bare bones” as far as overall presentation. It would be great if the instructions indicated that you can move & place the battlefield, instead of me finding out by accident after craning my neck.

Question: Would it be possible to give the user the option to scale down the entire battlefield for sitting 
comfort, and/or scale it up for standing play?

reviews.review_title: If I could give 6 stars I would.  BUY IT
reviews.review_description:
This game is amazing.   I bought it because I had some extra cash to spend and was looking for something to sit back and enjoy in VR.

I am 46 years old and this game just brings the inner child in me out.  I am sitting here giggling and laughing in glee as I defend from missiles and planes and helicopters.  I am the iron Dome Defense.

The way I can play in Mixed Reality is great, so I can keep an eye on my cat or watch the news as I defend my base.

Thank you to the devs for making this sweet little gem of a game.

reviews.review_title: nice but needs an easy mode
reviews.review_description:
After level 5, it gets really hard.  Would be fun to have a more relax mode.  Where i can play more then a few minutes.

A small campain with different cities and an ending would be nice.

developer_response.body: Hey, thanks for the feedback. Kind of a coincidence - we're actually working on that right now: a dynamic campaign with different cities and an ending. Stay tuned for updates.

reviews.review_description:
This is a super nice mixed reality remake of Missile command! And it is a perfect way of playing it with your hands! Vector graphics in the air in front of you! 
Maybe one of the best MR table games I have seen!
So you should have this one in your collection!

Now I do hope we would see similar game designs in MR for Defender, Asteroids, SpaceDuel, Gravatar, Frogger, Centipede and Galaga MR vector graphics remakes.
Such a simple concept but awesome execution! Instantly in my top 10. My only wish would be to be able to use the controllers instead of my fingers.

reviews.review_title: Classic 80s Arcade Action Comes to VR
reviews.review_description: I'm an 80s kid, and was fortunate enough to have the classic arcade experience. A big part of that experience was all things Atari, and especially the legendary game -- Missile Command! Here we are, in the year 2024, and many of the games of my youth simply haven't made it to VR in a way that's satisfying. Airspace Defender, a homage to Missile Command, changes all of that. Whether you've ever played Missile Command or not, I can 100% guarantee Airspace Defender will be one of your VR all-time favorites.

reviews.review_title: I’m enjoying it but need an Easy mode.
reviews.review_description:
Nice retro graphics, sound and overall feel. 
Would be nice to have an easy level because I’m finding it difficult to get ￼very far in the game.

reviews.review_title: It looks familiar, because it is.
reviews.review_description:
Yes, this is Missile Command in MR, viewed from a god perspective. Gone is the giant trackball and instead, you launch your weapons with a pinch (no controllers). The addition of a third dimension adds to the difficulty in predicting the trajectory, but gives it more depth. Longevity is helped by upgrades when you clear a level, whether you choose to upgrade weapons, or rebuild installations, is your choice.
The music has a John Carpenter feel to it, which is in keeping with the graphics, which are somewhat reminiscent of those in Escape From New York.
All in all, a solid, old-school, arcade blaster, completely reimagined in MR.
Challenging but still fun.

reviews.review_title: Awesome MR update on a classic
reviews.review_description:
The very definition of retro futuristic. An old school arcade classic, with a retro look, but delivered in a unique way in full 3D mixed reality. It's simple to play but difficult to master. Simplistic in style but beautiful and impressive in its own way. What an excellent idea and brilliantly executed to update the game this way.

Hand tracking works reasonably well but I do sometimes get misfires. Animation is nice and smooth. Personally I find the addition of depth in 3D makes it much harder to judge and would like to see a tutorial more dedicated to getting used to that. I'd also like it if there was a none-dome option and a resize ability.

As good as it is it is still a fairly limited experience so I think it could do with a rethink on pricing when compared with other titles which offer more, but it's hardly a bank breaker either. All in all it's a great little game and worth having.

For all of those who love a good a classic experience, I recommend this game 100 percent. It’s a refreshing change a pace given all the modern games on the market. It’s also extremely easy to pick up and before you know it you will be playing for hours trying to beat your last high score. Definitely worth your time. 
5/5 MASTAPEECE.

What a great concept! It brings the old Missile Command game into the modern era… more specifically the VR era.

Graphics have a retro feel with the line like vector graphics and neon green.

Sounds are good too, the loud Boom! Along with the bright flashes and explosions! Some of the best explosions in a VR game… Wow! Very immersive.

As you progress through the levels, you get to replace destroyed cities or upgrade your weapons… Gatling guns to drone launchers and faster missiles and rockets.

Compete against your friends scores with an online leaderboard.

It’s addictive and has that “just one more go” pull about it. An easy pick up and play game. You adjust the height of the playing field and away you go, using hand gestures as you play…. Which are very reactive and accurate.

Developer is active with this game and is bringing new updates in the future.

Thank you for this modern trip down memory lane!

The gameplay is an absolute blast! That and the visuals bring me back to the 80’s and the old Missile Command game on the Atari. So nostalgic! The difficulty is almost perfect for casual play with one exception: The hand tracking not seeing my gestures is usually where I get messed up. I think that this is partially due to the fatigue from awkwardly and repeatedly pinching the air. I think that I would have much less fatigue and would play the game for longer if I was pulling the trigger on the controllers instead of using hand tracking. I would also like to see a menu where I could turn the music volume down. Overall though this game is really fun and is my new go to game for just killing a bit of time

reviews.review_title: Very fun arcade classic remained for MR
reviews.review_description: This is based on missile command from the early days of gaming.  This game is really d**n fun but I do think it could be a bit cheaper as 16$ is a huge asking price for what you get. However the game is very polished, runs well, and is overall a fun game to play when you're wanting a arcade classic with a modern take

reviews.review_title: Old school game with new school feel
reviews.review_description:
A fun game with just the right amount of difficulty. I thought I'd be amazing at this based on my history playing old arcade games, but my poor citizens keep getting blown up.

Using hands and taking this into a 3D bubble is a fun twist on an old mechanic. Add in all the variety of attacks and I know I'll keep coming back.

Tells you in the beginning drop your controls . So I can’t play it . Doesn’t work at all on quest 2.

What a waste of money

developer_response.body: Hey. Sorry you've had a terrible experience, that sucks. What's the issue you're encountering? A large % of our audience is playing the game on Quest 2, including some of the TOP 10 players on the Leaderboards. Have you enabled Hand Tracking on your device? Please join our Discord so we can investigate and resolve your issue.

reviews.review_description: The game is smooth cool and fun￼

After completing the training levels I was so hooked that I decided to play for multiple hours to get on 1 of the top 10 leaderboards. Little did I know I'd make it on all 4 of the top 10 leaderboards! Games like this are exactly what mixed reality headsets were missing. I can now put on my headset just to play a quick game of Airspace Defender without worrying about sinking a ton of time or building up a sweat while still having a great time! I also hope to see more people in the Discord as this game gets better and better.

Airspace Defender is not your typical VR/MR game
it’s very unique, I have had  hours of fun clicking my fingers together, that’s right put those controllers down and use hand tracking.

Defend you entire city from enemy rockets, missiles and helicopter gun ships.

The concept is amazing and older gamers will revel in this.
The Mixed Reality works very well and hand tracking is on point.
Well done to Not Suspicious for making a cracking game.

I love this game, but I came here to say that I was inspired by Bill Carlton in the movie High Score, so it was so great to see the interview with him the other day. If you haven’t seen High Score, you can find it for free on the internet.

Basically, Bill Carlton is an amazing Missile Command player who decides he wants to set the high score, but his efforts are constantly derailed by technology. What I took away from the movie was Bill’s unbelievable preparation and determination to be the best in the world at something, and then his composure when things don’t go his way. It was super inspiring, and it’s a lesson everyone could learn. Honestly, the guy is a legend, and I wish I had his drive to be that good at anything.

Anyway, Airspace Defender is a mixed reality reimagining of the retro arcade classic Missile Command. It’s fantastic, and is a game I find myself frequently playing in short bursts. It uses hand tracking technology, and it’s the only game of its kind. I love it, and I think you will too. I hope it adds more levels in the future, but other than that, it’s really perfect at what it is. It’s not really a game I’ll play for long periods of time, but I’ll play a round just about every time I boot up the machine, and maybe one day, I’ll decide to set the world record. Well done.

At first it was hard to get used to since I’ve not played any games that use hand tracking. But it sure is fun and not easy. Have only got to level 5 so far before I die. Definitely worth the 15 bucks.
Pretty cool BUT you will need to go to qest settings and turn on hand tracking but cool fun game.

Absolutely amazing MR Hand Tracking game! 

This will have you playing for a long time it’s so addictive.

Airspace Defender VR sets the gold standard for immersive aerial combat games in virtual reality! From the moment you step into the cockpit, you’re transported into an intense, adrenaline-pumping battle for the skies. The game boasts stunning visuals, realistic aircraft physics, and breathtaking soundscapes that make every mission feel like a blockbuster movie come to life.

The controls are incredibly intuitive, offering both newcomers and seasoned pilots a seamless flying experience. Customizable fighter jets, dynamic weather effects, and a variety of thrilling mission objectives keep the gameplay fresh and engaging. Each dogfight is a heart-racing spectacle, pushing your skills and strategy to the limit.

What truly elevates Airspace Defender VR is its attention to detail—whether it’s the immersive HUD, the feeling of soaring through the clouds, or the intense explosions that light up the sky. This is more than a game; it’s an experience that makes you feel like a hero defending the world.

For anyone looking for the ultimate VR action game, Airspace Defender VR is a masterpiece that shouldn’t be missed. It’s a must-play and a shining example of what VR gaming can achieve!

I really like what has been done with this game, it's taking such a simple core concept, retaining everything that makes it a classic in the first place, but modernising and expanding on it all too.

The use of mixed reality adds a lot to the experience, as does the hand tracking, which are options that a lesser developer wouldn't have included or made focal points, so they deserve a lot of praise for that, on top of it just being a fun game that you can pick up for 10 minutes or an hour and inevitably have a good time.
The game is very entertaining and is very well done

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for the kind words! Is there anything we can do to earn the 5th star?! :D If so, join our Discord and let us know what else you'd like to see in-game!

reviews.review_description: Super fun and very addictive game! Trying to beat everyone on the leaderboards.
